title: Red Hat Bug Fix Advisory: scap-security-guide bug fix and enhancement update
overview: An update for scap-security-guide is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.
description: The SCAP Security Guide project provides a guide for configuration of the system from the final system's security point of view. The guidance is specified in the Security Content Automation Protocol (SCAP) format and constitutes a catalog of practical hardening advice, linked to government requirements where applicable. The project bridges the gap between generalized policy requirements and specific implementation guidelines.For detailed information on changes in this release, see the SCAP Security Guide release notes linked from the References section.
affected_products: Red Hat Enterprise Linux for x86_64 - Extended Update Support 10 x86_64
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for Power, little endian
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for ARM 64 - 4 years of updates
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for IBM z Systems - 4 years of updates
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for IBM z Systems

Red Hat Enterprise Linux Server for Power LE - Update Services for SAP Solutions
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for x86_64 - 4 years of updates 10 x86_64
Red Hat Enterprise Linux Server - AUS
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for x86_64 10 x86_64
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for Power, little endian - 4 years of support

fixes.description: "Ensure root path integrity" implementation is not complete
Rule "Ensure events that modify the system's networkenvironment are collected" is not handling /etc/NetworkManager
Rule "audit_rules_kernel_module_loading_create" isn't filtering on "auid!=unset" and similar
SCAP is considering rsyslog directives in RainerScript format as FAIL when multi-lined
Rule xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_sshd_use_approved_ciphers should not apply to RHEL8 as is
Ansible systemd_service task is invalid on RHEL 9.0
xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_accounts_user_interactive_home_directory_defined fails when a user logged in once in cockpit
Missing CIS Rule ?4.4.3.2.5 Ensure password maximum sequential characters is configured" (CIS Server Level 1 v3.0.0 profile)
Rebase scap-security-guide to the latest upstream version
[RHEL9] Rebase scap-security-guide to the latest upstream version
[RHEL8] Rebase scap-security-guide to the latest upstream version
Remediatin script for xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_ensure_logrotate_activated does not take effect

full_text:
An update for scap-security-guide is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

The SCAP Security Guide project provides a guide for configuration of the system from the final system's security point of view. The guidance is specified in the Security Content Automation Protocol (SCAP) format and constitutes a catalog of practical hardening advice, linked to government requirements where applicable. The project bridges the gap between generalized policy requirements and specific implementation guidelines.For detailed information on changes in this release, see the SCAP Security Guide release notes linked from the References section.
